source_pr.title: ddl, test : fix CI with adding new tk to execute additional sql.
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
Fix [CI problem](https://internal.pingcap.net/idc-jenkins/blue/rest/organizations/jenkins/pipelines/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/runs/15267/nodes/75/steps/278/log/?start=0) caused by #13119

### What is changed and how it works?
**Background**:
 In the ddl state change callback, we conduct additional sql to show create table, in order to make sure the schema state is the same with what we want it to be.  

**Root Cause** : 
This will cause `resetContextOfStmt` of the same `tk`, consequently, causing data race.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 - Integration test
 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Release note

 - fix CI with adding new tk to execute additional sql in `TestShowCreateTable`.

symptoms: This will cause `resetContextOfStmt` of the same `tk`, consequently, causing data race.
failure_signature: This will cause `resetContextOfStmt` of the same `tk`, consequently, causing data race.
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness is due to a real concurrency data race: the test executes additional SQL inside a DDL hook/callback while the main test continues, and reusing the same `testkit.TestKit` (`tk`) can concurrently call `resetContextOfStmt`, triggering `-race` failures. The PR stabilizes it by introducing a separate `tkInternal` dedicated to executing those additional SQL statements inside the callback, avoiding concurrent reuse of the same session/testkit.
fix_pattern: Avoid sharing a `TestKit`/session across concurrent goroutines/callbacks: create a separate `TestKit` (and session) for async hook SQL execution to prevent races like `resetContextOfStmt`.

evidence: evidence: This will cause `resetContextOfStmt` of the same `tk`, consequently, causing data race.
